figures.caption: Figure 1. We track the evolution of the Qwen3-4B-Base model throughout the training process. The recorded metrics include the entropy of the output distribution, the average probability of rank-k tokens (P̄rank-k), and the performance fluctuations on the evaluation datasets.
Figure 2. Comparison of rank-k trends across the transition from RLEP to DyJR.

Figure 3. (a) Pass@k performance on the Beyond AIME dataset across varying sampling budgets k. (b) and (c) Computational latency comparison between GRPO (w/o KL) and DyJR during the Rollout phase and the Actor Update phase (encompassing log-probability calculation, forward passes, and gradient updates), respectively.
Table 3. Experimental settings adopted in this work. We employ the AdamW optimizer with β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.95 and a weight decay of 0.1. The gradient clipping threshold is set to 1.0 for all models.

Table 1. Performance comparison on various mathematical benchmarks. Best results are bolded. We report the mean and standard deviation across three independent runs for GRPO and DyJR (denoted in blue parentheses). Note that Minerva* is reported as Mean@16, while all other benchmarks are evaluated using Mean@256.
